title: UK personal-loan borrower dashboard: balance, schedule, payments, receipts, early repayment
productContext: Fictional UK consumer credit web app (product name 'Lendflow') giving an active borrower a post-disbursement dashboard: outstanding balance, payment schedule, payment history with downloadable receipts, and an early-repayment option. Early repayment is regulated territory in the UK (partial early settlement rights under the Consumer Credit Act) and MUST be presented honestly: no early-repayment fee figures, no interest-savings calculations, and no settlement quote are supplied in this brief and must not be fabricated. The dashboard must clearly distinguish a SCHEDULED future payment from a TAKEN payment and must never present a forecast as a completed transaction. The product is fictional and is not FCA-authorised. Every URL in this brief is synthetic and uses example.com.
users: active borrower checking the outstanding balance and upcoming payment schedule
borrower interested in early repayment who wants an honest settlement quote without fabricated fee figures
borrower retrieving a receipt or statement for a payment that has already been taken
jobs: see my current outstanding balance and the next scheduled payment clearly distinguished from past payments
download a receipt or statement for a payment that has already been taken
explore early repayment honestly: see that a settlement quote is dynamic and that no fee or savings figure is fabricated
tell scheduled, taken, and failed payments apart at a glance
requiredJourneys: active borrower opens the dashboard and immediately sees the outstanding balance and the next scheduled payment, clearly marked as scheduled-not-yet-taken
borrower retrieves a downloadable receipt for a taken payment without confusion with a scheduled one
early-repayment-interested borrower reaches an honest settlement-quote entry that does not fabricate a fee, savings, or guaranteed figure
constraints: Scheduled future payments MUST be visually and semantically distinct from taken payments; presenting a scheduled payment as already-collected, or vice versa, is forbidden.

Early repayment MUST be presented honestly: no early-repayment fee, interest-savings figure, or settlement quote is supplied in this brief and none may be fabricated; the settlement figure must surface as a dynamic pull, not a hardcoded number.
Receipts and statements MUST be downloadable for taken payments and MUST NOT be generatable for scheduled-but-not-yet-taken payments.

No interest rate, APR, outstanding-balance value, payment-amount value, or settlement figure is supplied in this brief; these must surface as templated placeholders or dynamic pulls, not as fabricated figures.
Failed or returned payments must be surfaced honestly with a recovery path; presenting a failed payment as taken, or hiding a failure behind a generic 'all good' state, is forbidden.
The product name Lendflow is fictional; do not imply FCA authorisation, Consumer Credit Act compliance certification, or affiliation with any real lender or credit reference agency.

mobileRules: outstanding balance and next scheduled payment lead the view and the scheduled-vs-taken distinction is legible at the smallest breakpoint
primary 'view schedule' and 'early repayment' actions remain reachable without horizontal scroll
each row carries a clear scheduled / taken / failed tag that does not collapse away on a narrow viewport
the schedule remains operable in a single-handed portrait grip with rows tappable to reveal detail
no hardcoded fee or savings figure is shown while the settlement quote is pending; a clear 'requesting your live figure' treatment must lead
the confirm action sits separate from the quote-request action so a borrower cannot accidentally settle by tapping once
